ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pickled eggplant class; no Vietnamese-specific Cà Pháo entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers preserved aubergines broadly; no specific Vietnamese pickled small white eggplant sub-entry exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for pickled eggplant food product as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical condiment serving of pickled eggplant (Cà Pháo) is approximately 30 g, equivalent to 2–3 small pickled eggplant pieces served alongside rice or a Vietnamese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Pickled Eggplant — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (G00010571); supplemented by USDA FoodData Central Eggplant, pickled (NDB 11210) for secondary nutrients
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) taken directly from HPB record G00010571. Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, saturated fat, sugar) estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pickled eggplant and published literature on Vietnamese pickled vegetables, as HPB record does not report these fields. Sodium estimated at 1200 mg/100 g based on typical salt-cured pickled eggplant preparations consistent with the input record's own clinical note.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Vegetables — Analogous estimate based on pickled/fermented low-carbohydrate vegetables; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Vietnamese pickled eggplant (Cà Pháo) found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 15 based on analogous low-carbohydrate pickled vegetables (eggplant GI ~15, consistent with low-GI vegetable category in Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables). Carbohydrate content is 2.36 g/100 g; glycemic load per 30 g serving = (15 × 2.36 × 0.30) / 100 = 0.11, rounded to 0.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content (~1200 mg/100 g) from salt-curing pickling process
hypertension.guidance: Pickled eggplant (Cà Pháo) should be strictly limited or avoided by hypertensive individuals due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 1200 mg/100 g, which far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold; if consumed, restrict to a very small condiment portion (≤15 g) and rinse before eating to reduce surface sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100 g), low GI (~15), and high estimated dietary fibre (~2.5 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pickled eggplant has a very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index, making it suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as a small condiment portion; however, commercial preparations should be checked for added sugar, and the high sodium content remains a secondary concern for those with diabetic nephropathy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content (~1200 mg/100 g) raises cardiovascular risk; fat content is negligible
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Despite negligible fat and cholesterol, the very high sodium content of pickled eggplant poses a significant cardiovascular risk by contributing to elevated blood pressure; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional condiment only, limiting intake to no more than 10–15 g per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetable (solanine content) and high sodium from pickling brine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable containing solanine, which may exacerbate joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; the high sodium from pickling further worsens RA-related tissue swelling, and this food should be avoided or tested cautiously by RA patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pickled Eggplant (Cà Pháo) is a traditional Vietnamese condiment made from small, round white eggplants (Solanum torvum or Solanum macrocarpon varieties known as Cà Pháo) that are preserved in a salt brine, often with garlic, chili, and sometimes sugar. The pickling process involves submerging the whole or halved small eggplants in a concentrated salt solution for several days to weeks, resulting in a tangy, salty, slightly crunchy condiment with a firm texture and a characteristic sour-salty flavour profile. Cà Pháo Muối is a staple accompaniment in Vietnamese cuisine, typically served alongside steamed rice, fermented shrimp paste (mắm tôm), or braised pork dishes. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0 g fat, and 2.36 g carbohydrate. The fat content is confirmed zero by the HPB source for this preparation. The most clinically significant nutrient is sodium, estimated at approximately 1200 mg/100 g due to the salt-curing process, making this food unsuitable for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or sodium-restricted diets. The glycemic index is estimated to be very low (~15) given the minimal carbohydrate content, making it appropriate for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in small condiment quantities. Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable and may be of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to solanine content.

meal_context: served with mắm tôm (fermented shrimp paste)

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record G00010571 (confidence high for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate). Secondary nutrients (sodium, dietary fibre, sugar, saturated fat) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central pickled eggplant data and published literature, as HPB does not report these fields for this record. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is medium due to reliance on estimation for secondary nutrients and GI.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB record G00010571; this is consistent with the nature of pickled eggplant as a vegetable preparation with no added fat — confirmed zero per HPB FoodData source. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all 0.0 consistent with the zero fat value from HPB. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based food with no cholesterol. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for Vietnamese pickled eggplant (Cà Pháo) are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a specific entry as of 2026. Sodium estimated at 1200 mg/100 g based on typical salt-cured pickled vegetable preparations and consistent with the clinical note in the input record. Dietary fibre estimated at ~2.5 g/100 g based on raw eggplant fibre content (USDA) adjusted for pickling. Sugar estimated at ~1.0 g/100 g. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples / Baking ingredients' to 'Vegetables / Pickled vegetables' as this is a pickled vegetable condiment. The protein value of 10.7 g/100 g from HPB is unusually high for eggplant (raw eggplant ~1 g/100 g); this may reflect a data entry anomaly in the HPB source, but the value is preserved as reported from the approved source without modification per Veritas source policy.
